title: coleus blumei
intro: Coleus blumei, also known as Coleus aromaticus or Coleus forskohlii, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is native to tropical regions. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly the presence of forskolin, a compound extracted from its roots that has been used to lower blood pressure and as a heart stimulant. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer. Traditionally, Coleus blumei has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to treat a range of ailments, including high blood pressure, asthma, and skin conditions.
